# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure resources that use service endpoints for connectivity. Enable service endpoints for Storage and SQL on our VNet but allow public access as fallback. This provides flexibility for accessing resources from both Azure and on-premises during migration.
# Category: cloud_network_security

parameters:
  vnetName: 
    type: string
  subnets: 
    type: object   # Define the structure of the nested object here, this will depend on your actual requirements
  storageAccountName:
    type: string
  sqlServerName:
    type: string
resources:
- type: 'Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks'
  name: "[parameters('vnetName')]"
  apiVersion: '2018-04-01'
  location: "[resourceGroup().location]"
  properties: 
    addressSpace:
      addressPrefixes: 
        - "[parameters('subnets').addressPrefix]"   # The actual parameter value for this will depend on your configuration
    subnets: [for each subscription in parameters('subnets')]
      name: "[subscription.name]"
      properties: 
        addressPrefix: "[subscription.prefix]"
        serviceEndpoints:
          - 'Microsoft.Storage'   # Allowing access to Storage
          - 'Microsoft.Sql'       # Allowing access to SQL
- type: 'Microsoft.Storage/storageAccounts'
  name: "[parameters('storageAccountName')]"
  apiVersion: '2018-07-01'
  location: "[resourceGroup().location]"
  properties: 
    networkAcls:
      defaultAction: 'Allow'   # Allowing public access
      virtualNetworkRules: []
- type: 'Microsoft.Sql/servers'
  name: "[parameters('sqlServerName')]"
  apiVersion: '2018-06-01-preview'
  location: "[resourceGroup().location]"
  properties: 
    virtualNetworkRules: []   # This should have rules to allow the VNet for connectivity, but we haven't defined those here as it depends on your specific requirements
